items.title: FOI documents show vetting process for NT's controversial Crown representative

children.content: Months before a furore over his social media posts, documents show the federal government completed background checks on controversial NT Administrator David Connolly.

gallery.altText: A graphic shows a photo of David Connolly alongside a stack of paperwork.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic shows a photo of David Connolly alongside a stack of paperwork.

items.title: Industry 'alert but not alarmed' as native weevil develops a taste for cotton

children.content: Farmers and researchers notice a rise in bicoloured weevils feeding on cotton crops in the Northern Territory.

image.altText: Masses of bicoloured weevils on a cotton flower in the NT.

items.title: Indigenous solar projects to tackle diesel shortage concerns and halve power bills

children.content: Two remote Indigenous communities have attracted a combined $11 million in funding for solutions to tackle their fuel security and cost-of-living crises.

image.altText: A photo showing a person walking towards a tractor carrying solar panels surround by fence and car outside the fence

items.title: What Anzac Day means to those who have served Australia

children.content: Australians taking part in Anzac Day commemorations share stories of sacrifice and bravery, and what today means to them.

gallery.altText: Female army officer wearing hat and chin strap
Man with beard wearing light-coloured suit with medal attached looks serious
Man wearing glasses and suit jacket with military medals either side of chest

thumbnail.altText: Female army officer wearing hat and chin strap
Man with beard wearing light-coloured suit with medal attached looks serious
Man wearing glasses and suit jacket with military medals either side of chest

items.title: Former high-profile NT doctor to stand trial on rape, gross indecency charges

children.content: A former high-profile Northern Territory doctor charged with multiple sexual offences, including rape, has been committed to stand trial later this year.

image.altText: Picture of a doctor wearing a white lab coat with a stethoscope

items.title: 'Amazing' horse-led Anzac Day march tradition continues over two decades

children.content: Every year on Anzac Day, for more than two decades, Janette Wilson saddles up her Waler horse and leads the procession for the Darwin march, representing the light horsemen and their companions who served in World War I.

image.altText: A woman, smiling widely, standing next to a horse with a red poppy tucked behind its ear, in a horse pen.

items.title: 'Prince Harry is not speaking for the UK': The surprising comments made ahead of King Charles's US trip

children.content: Little more than a week after Meghan and Harry's "quasi royal" tour in Australia, King Charles will be conducting the real thing in America, with much higher stakes.

image.altText: Harry and Meghan greet people in line.

items.title: Thousands of Dutch families adopted the graves of people they never knew

children.content: In The Netherlands, a quiet commitment to honour the young soldiers who were buried thousands of kilometres from home has continued for 80 years.

gallery.altText: illustration of man and girl under red umberella looking at graves with dutch windmill and poppies
A black and white image of WWII soldiers in uniform posing in two rows for an official portrait

thumbnail.altText: illustration of man and girl under red umberella looking at graves with dutch windmill and poppies
A black and white image of WWII soldiers in uniform posing in two rows for an official portrait

items.title: Act before commodities boom leaves us bust, urges former Treasury boss

children.content: Former Treasury secretary Ken Henry says the under-taxed resources sector has hollowed out Australia's economy, and raising more revenue from the sale of LNG resources was in the national interest.

image.altText: A man in a suit and white shirt stares into the distance

items.title: Social licence test shows why Labor acts on NDIS but not gas

children.content: Labor's radical transformation of the NDIS as it resists a growing chorus of support for a new gas tax exposes the limit of its willingness to act when given a social licence.

image.altText: The prime minister looks at journalists.

items.title: There's no autocorrect here. It's quiz time

children.content: Delighted by diagraphs or hyped for homophones? Step inside and showcase your spelling skills.

image.altText: The comedian Guy Montgomery, a man in his mid-30s in a 70s tan suit on the set of his Spelling Bee

items.title: Sex workers speak out about being caught in the middle of PNG's HIV crisis

children.content: Workers have told the ABC about the battle to stay safe as the Pacific nation battles a rising number of infections.

image.altText: Two woman walk down the footpath of a city past cars

items.title: 'Waiting for Armageddon': Nimbin preppers ready if world goes up in smoke

children.content: It may not be the end of the world, but these Australians are finding creative ways to beat the cost-of-living crisis and improve their food security in troubled times.

gallery.altText: a man with a white beard smiles at the camera. He stands next to a red building
A woman stands behind jars of preserved food

thumbnail.altText: a man with a white beard smiles at the camera. He stands next to a red building
A woman stands behind jars of preserved food

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair smiles at the camera

items.title: Two out of three stolen cars are left with 'some form of hazard' in them

children.content: 'Opportunistic' car thieves are leaving vehicles littered with syringes, weapons and even traps, and in 2025, 77 per cent of vehicles inspected by one contractor had signs of methylamphetamine used at levels harmful to health.

image.altText: A man in a blue shirt sitting in front of a blue ute in his driveway.

items.title: Ollie Hollands 'in a good space' despite late omission after brother's struggles

children.content: Carlton coach Michael Voss says Elijah Hollands's younger brother Ollie is doing OK despite being a late out for the club's Anzac Day clash with Fremantle.

image.altText: Ollie Hollands points while on the field at Carlton AFL training.

items.title: In the aftermath of complex grief there can be post-traumatic growth

children.content: It's normal to feel distress when you go through trauma. But some people also experience personal growth to make positive changes in their lives.

gallery.altText: Image of Blake Johnston surfing a wave wearing a cowboy hat.

thumbnail.altText: Image of Blake Johnston surfing a wave wearing a cowboy hat.

items.title: At 85, Ringo Starr is still chasing his country music dream

children.content: Ringo's childhood love of classic country still influences the new music that The Beatles's drummer makes.

image.altText: Ringo Starr stands in an outdoor garden wearing sunglasses, t shirt and jacket. He holds up a peace sign to the camera

items.title: You can holiday for free at this remote island, but there's a catch

children.content: North West Island is one of five national parks where you can holiday in exchange for campground hosting duties.

image.altText: An aerial image of a green coral cay island surrounded by reef

items.title: Ancient and 'sterile' plant population found, but its location remains a secret

children.content: A second population of an ancient tree, King's lomatia, has been found in the south-west of Tasmania. A scientist says the precise location must be kept secret, or risk being 'killed by love'.

gallery.altText: Woman looks at a small plant in a nursery

thumbnail.altText: Woman looks at a small plant in a nursery

items.title: Russian attack on Ukrainian apartment block kills seven, injures dozens

children.content: An apartment block in the south-eastern city of Dnipro was struck twice by Russian drones, officials say.

image.altText: A young man stands with a foil blanket around him surrounded by rubble.

items.title: Housing developer Assemble slashes number of promised affordable homes

children.content: Victorian planning authorities will allow Assemble to significantly reduce the number of affordable homes offered at large developments in Melbourne's north by more than half.

items.title: Indigenous boxing gym developing 'self-belief, respect, discipline'

children.content: A Canberra Indigenous-led boxing camp is helping young fighters hone their skills for life inside and outside the ring in its first development camp, continuing a proud tradition of First Nations involvement in the sport.

gallery.altText: A young boy hits a punching bag in a boxing gym.
Two young boys sit side-by-side wearing boxing gloves in a gym.

thumbnail.altText: A young boy hits a punching bag in a boxing gym.
Two young boys sit side-by-side wearing boxing gloves in a gym.

items.title: Outback post office to stay open thanks to work of local women

children.content: Dirranbandi residents would have faced a two-hour car ride just to post a letter if it weren't for the work of these locals.

image.altText: Jane and Montana stand on the steps of the Rural Transaction Centre in Dirranbandi.

items.title: Footballer returns to field after 15 days in induced coma

children.content: A Gippsland footballer who spent 15 days in an induced coma after contracting a rare nerve condition has made a remarkable return to the field.

image.altText: A photo of a male football player in a white, blue, red and black singlet and blue football shorts, on a football field

items.title: 'Tombstone tourists' keep Australia's oldest surviving European cemetery alive

children.content: Nestled between apartment blocks and opposite an escape room, St John's Cemetery holds many unique stories from history.

gallery.altText: Ms Dunn wearing gloves and a hat holds a pair of garden shearers she is using to remove weeds from a grave.

thumbnail.altText: Ms Dunn wearing gloves and a hat holds a pair of garden shearers she is using to remove weeds from a grave.
